items.headline: City Council reviews new Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: Mountain View is developing a draft plan to improve biodiversity and urban forest management across the city. The study session on April 28 will allow council members to review and discuss the plan before potential adoption.

items.headline: Council to decide future of middle school athletics program management
items.summary: City Council will discuss how to manage Palo Alto's middle school athletics program going forward, including potential changes to the current model. This affects youth sports for local families.

items.headline: Foothills Nature Preserve improvements project gets detailed review
items.summary: Council will receive a detailed update on the Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project (PE-21000), which involves physical enhancements to a popular local natural area.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes and retail on 4.22 acres
items.summary: A new project would bring 55 townhomes and 12 apartments plus 18,535 square feet of retail space to Sunnyvale, including a new two-story retail building alongside the existing Speedy's Tacos. The Planning Commission held a study session on April 27 to review the special development permit and subdivision plan.

items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council is updating business license tax rates through a municipal code amendment. The specific rate changes and their impact on local businesses will be determined during the council's deliberation.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Large signs approved for Levi's Stadium area
items.summary: Santa Clara approved temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The signs will be allowed for a limited period surrounding the special events.

items.headline: Milpitas approves FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: The City Council approved updated development fees and user charges for the fiscal year starting July 2026. These fees affect costs for residents and businesses seeking permits and city services.

items.headline: Santa Clara County homelessness plan presented to Milpitas council
items.summary: The County of Santa Clara presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness to the City Council. This regional initiative may influence local policies and services related to homelessness in Milpitas.
